leadText: The Dire Hit (Japanese: クリティカット Criticut, known as クリティカッター Criticutter prior to Generation V and in Hisui) is a type of battle item introduced in Generation I. It temporarily boosts a Pokémon's critical hit ratio.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generation I 1.2 Generation II 1.3 Generation III onward Effect [ edit | edit source ] Dire Hit replicates the effect of the move Focus Energy , which itself has changed through the generations. It does not stack with Focus Energy or other Dire Hits. Generation I [ edit | edit source ] When used on a Pokémon from the Bag during battle, the Pokémon's chance of getting a critical hit is reduced by 75% (due to a glitch) while it is in battle. Generation II [ edit | edit source ] When used on a Pokémon from the Bag during battle, it raises that Pokémon's critical hit ratio by 1 stage while it is in battle. It also increases the Pokémon's friendship a little. This improves the default critical hit odds from 17/256 to 1/8. Generation III onward [ edit | edit source ] When used on a Pokémon from the Bag during battle, it raises that Pokémon's critical hit ratio by 2 stages while it is in battle. It also increases the Pokémon's friendship a little. This improves the default critical hit odds from 1/16 to 1/4 (Gen. III - V ), from 1/16 to 1/2 (Gen. VI ), or from 1/24 to 1/2 (Gen. VII +).

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Modern Games Description Stad Temporarily heightens the probability of scoring critical hits in one battle. G S C Ups critical hit ratio. (1 BTL) R S E Colo XD Raises the critical-hit ratio during one battle. FR LG Raises the critical-hit ratio of Pok é mon in battle. Wears off if the Pok é mon is withdrawn. D P Pt HG SS An item that raises the critical-hit ratio of a Pokémon in battle. It wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn. B W B2 W2 It raises the critical-hit ratio greatly. It can be used only once and wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn. X Y OR AS S M US UM An item that raises the critical-hit ratio greatly. It can be used only once and wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn. P E Sw Sh BD SP LA An item that greatly raises the critical-hit ratio of a Pokémon during a battle. It can be used only once and wears off if the Pokémon is withdrawn. S V An item that can be used once to sharply boost the critical-hit ratio of a Pokémon in battle. This effect disappears if the Pokémon leaves the battle. Hisui Games Description LA An item that sharply boosts the critical-hit ratio of a Pokémon during a battle. This effect disappears if the Pokémon leaves the battle. S V An item that sharply boosts the critical-hit ratio of a Pokémon in battle. This effect disappears if the Pokémon leaves the battle.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Masters Dire Hit: Raises the user's critical-hit rate by one stat rank. Dire Hit +: Raises the user's critical-hit rate by two stat ranks. Dire Hit All: Raises the critical-hit rate of all allied sync pairs. Dire Hit All +: Raises the critical-hit rate of all allied sync pairs by two stat ranks.

sections.title: In the core series games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 Price 1.2 Effect 1.2.1 Generation I 1.2.2 Generation II 1.2.3 Generation III onward 1.3 Wonder Launcher 1.4 Description 1.5 Acquisition In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] Price [ edit | edit source ] Games Cost Sell price R G B Y / R B Y G S C R S E FR LG D P Pt HG SS B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS P E BD SP $ 650 $ 325 S M US UM Sw Sh $ 1,000 $ 500 LA N/A $ 400 S V $ 1,000 $ 250 At Join Avenue 's Market, Dire Hit can be bought in dozens at $ 5850 each, which equates to $ 487.5 per Dire Hit. Effect [ edit | edit source ] Dire Hit replicates the effect of the move Focus Energy , which itself has changed through the generations. It does not stack with Focus Energy or other Dire Hits. Generation I [ edit | edit source ] When used on a Pokémon from the Bag during battle, the Pokémon's chance of getting a critical hit is reduced by 75% (due to a glitch) while it is in battle. Generation II [ edit | edit source ] When used on a Pokémon from the Bag during battle, it raises that Pokémon's critical hit ratio by 1 stage while it is in battle. It also increases the Pokémon's friendship a little. This improves the default critical hit odds from 17/256 to 1/8. Generation III onward [ edit | edit source ] When used on a Pokémon from the Bag during battle, it raises that Pokémon's critical hit ratio by 2 stages while it is in battle. It also increases the Pokémon's friendship a little. This improves the default critical hit odds from 1/16 to 1/4 (Gen. III - V ), from 1/16 to 1/2 (Gen. VI ), or from 1/24 to 1/2 (Gen. VII +). Wonder Launcher [ edit | edit source ] The Dire Hit appears as three Wonder Launcher items, increasing the critical hit ratio by 1, 2, or 3 stages. It costs 3, 5, or 7 points to use respectively.
